title: Workflow Syntax, Expressions, YAML, and Data Contracts · Delivery-System Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for workflow syntax, expressions, yaml, and data contracts and requires the learner to defend the smallest safe validated workflow data contract. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for validated workflow data contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Author name, on, permissions, concurrency, env, defaults, jobs, and job-level contracts with explicit supported syntax.
Control YAML scalars, booleans, quoting, multiline blocks, reserved-looking keys, indentation, and serialization boundaries.
Reason about expression literals, loose equality, coercion, truthiness, functions, object filters, fromJSON, and toJSON without leaking data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Workflow Schema Top Level
steps.instruction:
Order the Ci Workflow Schema Top Level delivery-system evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Workflow Schema Top Level.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Ci Workflow Schema Top Level assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Workflow Schema Top Level, keep Ci Workflow Schema Top Level connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: A quiz workflow parses YAML but treats false as text, expands a multiline value unsafely, uses an unavailable context, and accepts a misspelled input. Validate syntax, types, timing, shell boundaries, and a changed event fixture. Competency probe: Author name, on, permissions, concurrency, env, defaults, jobs, and job-level contracts with explicit supported syntax.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Ci Workflow Schema Top Level has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Workflow Schema Top Level, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Workflow Schema Top Level precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Validate syntax, types, timing, shell boundaries, and a changed event fixture. Competency probe: Author name, on, permissions, concurrency, env, defaults, jobs, and job-level contracts with explicit supported syntax.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Ci Workflow Schema Top Level, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Valid YAML is necessarily a valid and intended GitHub Actions workflow.

options.text: Capture event, workflow and revision identity, actor, permissions, runner, toolchain, dependency, artifact, environment, and release state for the quiz Ci Workflow Schema Top Level case
